title: When Analyzing Injustice: - Ask:
subtitle: Question the Fairness Narrative
description:
When analyzing injustice:
- Ask: "Is this outcome truly deserved, or am I rationalizing it?"
- Empathize: Consider factors outside the victim’s control.
- Acknowledge reality: The world isn’t always fair—avoid blaming victims.

Example: If someone loses their job unexpectedly, avoid assuming they must have done something wrong.

howTo.title: Identify the unjust outcome
howTo.summary: Reflect on a recent situation where someone appeared to 'get what they deserved.' Write down the event and your initial attribution to set a baseline for your judgment.

howTo.title: Conduct a fairness check
howTo.summary: Use the six quick checks to analyze the situation. Identify situational factors, request missing data, and consider counterfactuals to gather evidence beyond your initial judgment.

howTo.title: Log your findings
howTo.summary: Record your revised confidence level and any new insights gained from the fairness check. This helps track changes in your understanding and reduces biases in future judgments.

howTo.title: Reflect on your learning
howTo.summary: At the end of the week, review your fairness checks. Note patterns in your judgments and how your initial attributions changed after gathering evidence.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and reinforce the habit of questioning assumptions about fairness. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/question-fairness-bias/.
